STATE OF CALIFORNIA -- Business, Transportation and Housing Agency                    PETE WILSON, Governor

DEPARTMENT OF CORPORATIONS


                                                                    IN REPLY REFER TO:

Los Angeles, California                                            FILE NO. ________
May 3, 1991




Mr. John Wallace
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95804

Subject: REQUEST FOR OPINION

Dear Mr. Wallace:

     This is a follow-up to my letter of April 26, 1991 for
a request for opinion on whether a scholarship donated
by a state-chartered credit union is subject to the Political
Reform Act, any other act administered by the Fair Political
Practices Commission, or any other known act of this State.

     As described in the National Institute for State Credit Union
Examination ("NISCUE") brochure, any person may contribute a
scholarship to NISCUE. The scholarship may be designated to a
specific examiner or generally to an examiner of an agency as
recipient. The scholarship donation will be made directly to
NISCUE.

     Any California state-chartered credit union scholarship donation
will be made directly to NISCUE and designated for an examiner of
the Department of Corporations. The Department of Corporations
will not accept a scholarship donation which is designated for a
specific examiner. The recipient of the scholarship will be
selected by an Executive staff member of the Department, namely
the Assistant Commissioner of the Financial Services Division.
The scholarship donation covers tuition, room and board and
provides a travel allowance. The scholarship donation may cover
all or a part of travel expenses. Any travel expenses not
covered by a scholarship will be paid by the Department of
Corporations.




LOS ANGELES 90010-3001          SACRAMENTO 95814-3860           SAN DIEGO 92101-3697         SAN FRANCISCO 94102-5303
3700 WILSHIRE BOULEVARD          1115 11TH STREET                1350 FRONT STREET            1390 MARKET STREET
(213) 736-2741                   (916) 445-7205                  (619) 237-7341               (415) 557-3787

Mr. John Wallace
Re: REQUEST FOR OPINION
May 3, 1991
Page 2


As described in the enclosed brochure, NISCUE is an educational
foundation sponsored by the National Association of State Credit
Union Supervisors ("NASCUS"). State regulators of credit unions
compose the membership of NASCUS. State chartered credit unions
may also join NASCUS as an Associate Member.

Your early consideration of this request will be appreciated.
The next NISCUE school is scheduled for early July 1991.

If you have any questions, please feel free to call Chief
Examiner Louisa Broudy at 8-677-3462 or me at 8-677-2776.

Very truly yours,

[illegible signature]
R. M. MURAKAMI
Assistant Commissioner
ATSS (8-677-2776)

RMM:bf

CC: L. A. Broudy, Chief Examiner

STATE OF CALIFORNIA -- Business, Transportation and Housing Agency

PETE WILSON, Governor

DEPARTMENT OF CORPORATIONS

Los Angeles, California
April 26, 1991

IN REPLY REFER TO:

FILE NO. _______

Mr. John Wallace
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Wallace:

Enclosed per our telephone conversation are a pamphlet and letter
distributed by the National Institute for State Credit Union
Examination ("NISCUE").

The National Association of State Credit Union Supervisors
("NASCUS") membership is composed of state credit union
regulators. A credit union may join NASCUS as an associate
member, see enclosed pamphlet.

Please call me if you have any questions at 8-677-2776.

Very truly yours,

R. M. MURAKAMI
Assistant Commissioner

RMM:bf
Enclosures

LOS ANGELES 90010-3001          SACRAMENTO 95814-3860          SAN DIEGO 92101-3697          SAN FRANCISCO 94102-5303
3700 WILSHIRE BOULEVARD          1115 11TH STREET                1350 FRONT STREET            1390 MARKET STREET
(213) 736-2741                  (916) 445-7205                 (619) 237-7341               (415) 557-3787

NISCUE

November, 1990

Dear 1990 NASCUS Conference Participant:

During your attendance at the 25th Annual NASCUS Conference a few
months ago, we shared with you some very important information
about the National Institute for State Credit Union Examination
(NISCUE) and its scholarship programs.

As the educational foundation of the National Association of
State Credit Union Supervisors (NASCUS), NISCUE strives to
preserve the dual-chartering system through quality training
programs. With the entire credit union industry under the
spotlight of Congressional scrutiny, GAO and the Treasury
scrutiny and Executive branch examination, improvements in the
skills of the examiners and the quality of the examination
program has become a number one priority for the NISCUE Board of
Trustees.

We ask that you join NISCUE in its effort to strengthen the
credit union industry by making a scholarship contribution.

Your contribution may be in any amount. If you prefer to
contribute for a specific individual or state and donate at least
$1,250, we will be happy to provide the specified individual or
state with a designated scholarship. $1,250 covers tuition, room
and board, and travel costs for one person.

We hope that you will participate in this vitally important
program. If you should have any questions regarding NISCUE or
the scholarship programs, please don't hesitate to contact
Sunye Kwon at (202) 582-9657.

Sincerely,

Lawrence C. Williams
Lawrence C. (Sparky) Williams, Alabama
Chairman

SK/sk

National Institute for
State Credit Union Examination

1901 North Fort Myer Drive • Suite 201 • Arlington, Virginia 22209 • phone (703) 528-8351
